old_value: {"id":"project-LICHEN-2auf.35.4","title":"Restore C signed link TX and atomic identity","description":"Review of 2auf.35 found lichen_link_tx returns EPROTONOSUPPORT whenever a legacy link key is loaded, disabling production TX, while rewritten C tests only assert that early failure. It also found lichen_link_copy_identity locks reads but lichen_link_load_key writes key state without that lock. Remove the false encryption inference, restore successful signed TX/RX/tamper/replay/buffer tests, and synchronize key publication/copying without holding locks during expensive derivation.","acceptance_criteria":"Loading retained legacy key state does not disable TX; signed C TX/RX succeeds; tamper/replay/buffer failure paths are independently tested; key publication and identity copy use the same lock with no torn identity; focused C and Zephyr tests plus three reviews pass.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"me@mark.atwood.name","created_at":"2026-07-16T23:23:22.645371Z","created_by":"Mark Atwood","updated_at":"2026-07-16T23:23:22.645371Z","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"project-LICHEN-2auf.35.4","depends_on_id":"project-LICHEN-2auf.35","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-07-16T23:23:22.783569Z","created_by":"Mark Atwood","metadata":"{}"}]}

{"id":"project-LICHEN-2auf.35.4","title":"Restore C signed link TX and atomic identity","description":"Review of 2auf.35 found lichen_link_tx returns EPROTONOSUPPORT whenever a legacy link key is loaded, disabling production TX, while rewritten C tests only assert that early failure. It also found lichen_link_copy_identity locks reads but lichen_link_load_key writes key state without that lock. Remove the false encryption inference, restore successful signed TX/RX/tamper/replay/buffer tests, and synchronize key publication/copying without holding locks during expensive derivation.","acceptance_criteria":"Loading retained legacy key state does not disable TX; signed C TX/RX succeeds; tamper/replay/buffer failure paths are independently tested; key publication and identity copy use the same lock with no torn identity; focused C and Zephyr tests plus three reviews pass.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"Mark Atwood","owner":"me@mark.atwood.name","created_at":"2026-07-16T23:23:22.645371Z","created_by":"Mark Atwood","updated_at":"2026-07-17T04:31:57.773232Z","started_at":"2026-07-17T04:31:57.773232Z","lease_expires_at":"2026-07-17T04:36:57.773232Z","heartbeat_at":"2026-07-17T04:31:57.773232Z","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"project-LICHEN-2auf.35.4","depends_on_id":"project-LICHEN-2auf.35","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-07-16T23:23:22.783569Z","created_by":"Mark Atwood","metadata":"{}"}]}
new_value: {"notes":"Production fix complete locally: retained link key no longer implies encryption; key publication/copy/signing use atomic snapshots; TX, announce, and app identity no longer read torn keys; snapshots securely clear; link_crypto tests now exercise successful retained-key signed TX/RX, payload/MIC tamper, replay non-publication, short-buffer ordering, and snapshot clear. Host frame/Schnorr CTest pass and three scoped reviews found no remaining issue. Required Linux Zephyr native_sim runtime is blocked because AWS profile personal expired and requires interactive 'aws login'; Docker unavailable."}
